blocked_items.title: AI-Native Workflow Platforms Win Mega-RIA Adoption as Jump and Avantos Raise $105M Combined
blocked_items.reason: Fabrication detected: Headline states '$105M Combined' but source shows $25M (Avantos Series A) + $80M (Jump Series B) = $105M total — this arithmetic is consistent, but the headline number '$105M' does not appear verbatim in the source text. However, this is not a contradiction since the components sum correctly.; Summary states Jump 'secured adoption across major RIAs and custodians including Merit and Focus Financial Partners' — source confirms this, no issue.; Summary states 'AI agents that eliminate swivel-chairing' — source text uses 'swivel‑chairing' verbatim, consistent.; Summary claims Jump raised '$80M Series B in the same week' — source confirms '$80 million Series B funding', consistent.; The summary and 'so what' reference 'Focus Financial Partners' as a Jump customer — source confirms this.; KEY ISSUE: The 'so what' section states '$80M raise across Focus Financial and major custodians' — but the source indicates broker-dealers LPL, Cetera, and Osaic have partnerships with Jump, and custodians are not explicitly named for Jump. Focus Financial is an RIA aggregator, not a custodian. This is a mischaracterization but not a direct number contradiction.; QUOTED PHRASE ISSUE: The summary states the capability 'is explicitly expanding toward AI-generated product recommendations for clients' — the source does say Ramadi 'expects will eventually expand into AI-generated product recommendations for clients', but the entry presents this as 'explicitly expanding' which overstates the source's 'expects will eventually expand'.; CRITICAL: The summary states Jump's $80M raise with 'identical AI-native operating system positioning' — source confirms both companies used this phrase, consistent.; The key stat '$96B' does NOT appear verbatim in the source. The source states 'the $96 billion AUM mega-RIA Mercer Advisors' — '$96B' is an abbreviation, not a verbatim match, though it represents the same figure. This is near-verbatim and not a contradiction.; FAIL TRIGGER: Summary states Avantos is 'live with Mercer Advisors ($96B AUM), Vanguard, SEI, and Guardian Life' as if all are clients — but source indicates Vanguard, SEI, and Guardian Life are both investors AND users, while the entry conflates investor/client roles without distinguishing them. This is not a number fabrication but a representational issue.; No outright number contradictions found for headline figures ($25M, $80M, $105M combined, $96B AUM).

blocked_items.title: Origin Launches AI Robo-Planner Targeting the 120M+ Underserved US Households Priced Out of Human Advice
blocked_items.reason: Fabrication detected: Headline states '120M+ Underserved US Households' but the source text explicitly states 'more-than-130 million households in the US', contradicting the 120M+ figure in the headline.; Summary states '$2,500–$10,000+ annual minimums' — source says '$2,500-$3,000 per year' minimum with 'many advisors charging annual minimums of $10,000 or more', which is broadly consistent but the framing '–$10,000+' slightly compresses the source's range.; Key stat in entry header says '130M+' which matches the source, but the headline uses '120M+' which contradicts the source's '130 million households' figure.; The summary phrase 'approximately 100,000 CFP professionals can serve only ~10 million households against a US base of 130+ million' is consistent with the source, but the headline's '120M+' directly contradicts the source's '130 million' figure for the same metric.

blocked_items.title: Orion Deepens Anthropic Integration as Claude Launches FactSet-Backed Financial Analysis Agent
blocked_items.reason: Fabrication detected: The summary and headline refer to 'Claude Cowork platform' — the source text says 'Claude Cowork agent', which is close, but more critically the entry calls it a 'platform' while the source says 'agent'. Minor but worth noting.; The key stat '2000' does NOT appear literally in the source. The source says 'some 2,000 attendees' — this is a near-verbatim match with a comma, so this is acceptable.; The summary states Anthropic 'launched new AI agent tools for its Claude Cowork platform targeting financial analysis and HR workflows' — the source says 'launched new artificial-intelligence tools for its Claude Cowork agent that target work in areas like financial analysis and human resources' — this is a near-verbatim paraphrase and acceptable.; The quoted phrase attributed to Orion's 'stated focus' — 'building a secure, enterprise-grade platform that harnesses these AI advances for its clients' — does NOT appear verbatim in the source. The source quote from Reed Colley is: 'What we're really focused on for our clients is harnessing the power of these [AI] changes and building a platform that's secure, enterprise grade, and built for privacy …' The summary fabricates a condensed paraphrase and presents it as a 'stated focus' with language not matching the source verbatim, but this is a paraphrase rather than a direct quote in quotation marks.; The company name 'Orion Advisor Solutions' does not appear in the source. The source refers only to 'Orion' without the full legal name 'Orion Advisor Solutions'.; '2,000' appears in the source as 'some 2,000 attendees' — the key stat of 2000 is supported.; The 'so what' references '2,000 RIA practitioners' — the source says '2,000 attendees', not specifically 'RIA practitioners'. This is a minor fabrication of specificity.; The 'so what' says 'year-plus Anthropic relationship' — the source says 'working with Orion for over a year', which supports this claim.; CRITICAL: The company name 'Orion Advisor Solutions' does not appear in the source text. The source only uses 'Orion'. While this may be the full legal name, the source does not use it — flagged as a potential fabrication of the full name, though this is a borderline issue.; The headline refers to 'FactSet-Backed Financial Analysis Agent' — the source confirms FactSet Research Systems as a partner and financial analysis as a target area, so this is supported.; No number appears in the headline itself that would fail.
